title: solidago virgaurea proles minuta
intro: Solidago virgaurea proles minuta, also known as Dwarf Goldenrod, is a subspecies of the Solidago virgaurea plant, a flowering perennial native to Europe and Western Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and soothing digestive problems, making it a popular herbal remedy in folk medicine. In herbal preparations, Dwarf Goldenrod is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into topical creams and salves for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects.
